title: Molten Slovakian Potato Dumplings
description: A romantic Slovakian dish featuring fermented potato starch, this ovo-vegetarian friendly recipe is perfect for a cozy night in. The namul-blanch-season process adds a unique flavor to the dish.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the fermented potato starch, all purpose flour, and a pinch of salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, and melted butter.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in a preheated oven at 375 degrees for 15 minutes, or until the dumplings are cooked through and lightly browned.
While the dumplings are baking, prepare the namul-blanch-season process by boiling a pot of water and blanching the chopped chives for 30 seconds.
Remove the chives from the water with a slotted spoon and season with salt and black pepper.
Serve the molten dumplings hot, topped with the seasoned chives and a pat of butter.
